docs.title: Citadel Wiki
docs.text: Welcome to the Citadel Wiki — a knowledge base built with MkDocs Material and published with GitHub Pages.

docs.title: What's here
docs.text:
• Getting Started — how to edit and add pages
  • About — about this wiki

Editing this wiki

Every page has a pencil icon in the top right that takes you straight to the source Markdown file on GitHub. Edit it there, or edit files under docs/ and push to main — the site rebuilds automatically.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
The Citadel Wiki is a static knowledge base.

  • Generator: MkDocs with the Material theme
  • Source: github.com/ihavedna/citadel-wiki
  • Hosting: GitHub Pages, deployed via GitHub Actions

docs.title: Why not MediaWiki?
docs.text: MediaWiki requires a PHP server and a SQL database, which GitHub Pages (a static host) cannot provide. MkDocs Material gives a wiki-like experience — navigation, search, and browser-based editing — while building to plain static files that Pages can serve.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This wiki is built from Markdown files in the docs/ directory. Every push to the main branch rebuilds and redeploys the site automatically via GitHub Actions.

docs.title: Adding a page
docs.text:
1. Create a new Markdown file under docs/, e.g. docs/lore.md.
  2. Add it to the nav: list in mkdocs.yml so it appears in the navigation.
  3. Commit and push to main.

docs.title: Editing a page
docs.text: Click the pencil icon at the top of any page to edit its source directly on GitHub, or edit the file under docs/ locally and push.

docs.title: Previewing locally
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

Then open http://localhost:8000 in your browser. The preview reloads as you edit.

docs.title: Building locally
docs.text:
mkdocs build

This generates the static site into the site/ directory.
